documentation.validate_training: If true, validate the training using a separate set of image/caption pairs, and log the results as `loss/val`. The curve will trend downwards as the model trains, then flatten and start to trend upwards as effective training finishes and the model begins to overfit the training data. Very useful for preventing overfitting, for checking if your learning rate is too low or too high, and for deciding when to stop training.
documentation.val_split_mode: Either 'automatic' or 'manual', ignored if validate_training is false. 'automatic' val_split_mode picks a random subset of the training set (the number of items is controlled by auto_split_proportion) and removes them from training to use as a validation set. 'manual' val_split_mode lets you provide your own folder of validation items (images+captions), specified using 'manual_data_root'.
documentation.auto_split_proportion: For 'automatic' val_split_mode, how much of the train dataset that should be removed to use for validation. Typical values are 0.15-0.2 (15-20% of the total dataset). Higher is more accurate but slower.
documentation.manual_data_root: For 'manual' val_split_mode, the path to a folder containing validation items.
documentation.extra_manual_datasets: Dictionary of 'name':'path' pairs defining additional validation datasets to load and log. eg { 'santa_suit': '/path/to/captioned_santa_suit_images', 'flamingo_suit': '/path/to/flamingo_suit_images' }
documentation.stabilize_training_loss: If true, stabilize the train loss curves for `loss/epoch` and `loss/log step` by re-calculating training loss with a fixed random seed, and log the results as `loss/train-stabilized`. This more clearly shows the training progress, but it is not enough alone to tell you if you're overfitting.
documentation.stabilize_split_proportion: For stabilize_training_loss, the proportion of the train dataset to overlap for stabilizing the train loss graph. Typical values are 0.15-0.2 (15-20% of the total dataset). Higher is more accurate but slower.
documentation.every_n_epochs: How often to run validation (1=every epoch, 2=every second epoch; 0.5=twice per epoch, 0.33=three times per epoch, etc.).
documentation.seed: The seed to use when running validation and stabilization passes.
documentation.use_relative_loss: logs val/loss as negative relative to first pre-train val/loss value
documentation.clip_score_enabled: If true, compute CLIP score (cosine similarity between one-step x̂₀ image reconstruction and the caption) during the normal validation pass. No image generation is required. Logged as val/{dataset}_clip_score. Higher is better; ~0.25-0.35 is typical for well-aligned image-text pairs.
documentation.clip_score_model: HuggingFace model ID for the CLIP model used for scoring. Default 'openai/clip-vit-base-patch32'. Cached after first download.
documentation.fdd_enabled: If true, compute Fréchet DINOv2 Distance (FDD) between fully-generated images and real validation images. Requires running full denoising inference (like anomaly eval). Logged as val/{dataset}_fdd. Lower is better; requires scipy.
documentation.fdd_n_images: Number of images to generate and compare for FDD. Higher gives a more stable estimate but takes longer. Default 256. Meaningful minimum is ~64; stable above ~128.
documentation.fdd_model: HuggingFace model ID for the DINOv2 feature extractor used in FDD. Default 'facebook/dinov2-small' (~22M params, fast). Can also use 'facebook/dinov2-base' or 'facebook/dinov2-large' for richer features.
